characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Practically the face of Mythbusters, Adam Savage is the more vocal, impulsive and energetic of the two main hosts, and was hired out ofIndustrial Light & Magicas a counter-point to Jamie. Adam's crafting style is messy and irreverent, perhaps even overly-complex, but this dovetails very nicely with Jamie's building sensibilities. While the two had a complicated relationship, Adam stating afterwards that they were "not friends", Adam emphasizes that their working relationship was wonderfully collaborative and resulted in their best work. While he and Jamie bowed out for Season 10, Adam returned as the host ofMythbusters Junior(although he notably wasnota Mythbuster for that show, as the kids were doing that role). Awesomeness by Analysis: In a few episodes, Adam is given the task of figuring out the measurements of certain props or rigs just by examining video footage. According to Jamie, he's well-known in the industry for his ability to do this. It's also worth noting that the one challenge in the "UltimateMythBuster" episode that he decisively bested Jamie at was guessing the weight of various items. Blind Without 'Em: Adam claims this in an early episode dealing with whether or not a regular ceiling fan can decapitate someone. When they ran the final test with a highly modified fan, Adam put his glasses on the ballistics-gel target headnotemade with a casting of Adam's headand then announced that the rest of the team would have to tell him what happens because "I can't see a damn thing". And Kari jokes saying if the gel head were alive, it wouldn’t be able to see with his “Coke-bottles” on. Brief Accent Imitation: A running gag during the exposition segments is for him to impersonate someone or something (like a walrus).

characters.name: Adam Savage
characters.description: Practically the face of Mythbusters, Adam Savage is the more vocal, impulsive and energetic of the two main hosts, and was hired out ofIndustrial Light & Magicas a counter-point to Jamie. Adam's crafting style is messy and irreverent, perhaps even overly-complex, but this dovetails very nicely with Jamie's building sensibilities. While the two had a complicated relationship, Adam stating afterwards that they were "not friends", Adam emphasizes that their working relationship was wonderfully collaborative and resulted in their best work. While he and Jamie bowed out for Season 10, Adam returned as the host ofMythbusters Junior(although he notably wasnota Mythbuster for that show, as the kids were doing that role). Awesomeness by Analysis: In a few episodes, Adam is given the task of figuring out the measurements of certain props or rigs just by examining video footage. According to Jamie, he's well-known in the industry for his ability to do this. It's also worth noting that the one challenge in the "UltimateMythBuster" episode that he decisively bested Jamie at was guessing the weight of various items. Blind Without 'Em: Adam claims this in an early episode dealing with whether or not a regular ceiling fan can decapitate someone. And Kari jokes saying if the gel head were alive, it wouldn’t be able to see with his “Coke-bottles” on. characters.name: Jamie Hyneman
characters.description: The much more stoic and reserved member of the M5 duo, Jamie has a past that isShrouded in Myth, both in real life and as a result of theExpansion Pack PastAdam loves to give him (whenever Jamie does mention something absurd he did, it's frequently hard to tell if he's joking or not). Jamie is very orderly and acted as the team leader, but despite his stoicism he can often surprise by taking simple build concepts to truly terrifying extremes. Jamie is also famous for his iconic appearance that's frequently compared to a walrus, given his mustache and beret combo. As Adam liked to say, while Adam "might be that guy from that show", Jamie is "definitely that guy from that show". Animal Motifs: The showreallygot its mileage by comparing Jamie to the animal namesake of his famous "walrus mustache". Bullet-Proof Fashion Plate: Multiple members of the cast and crew have expressed bafflement at how he almost always manages to keep his white shirt spotless (to the point that any instances of it actually getting stained are often made a big deal of). Turns out he actually was using (and continues to use) a special clothing treatment that basically makes any fabric it's applied to impervious to most liquids,as well as keeping a bunch of extra clean shirts on hand. The Comically Serious: The show gets a lot of mileage juxtaposing his stoic demeanor and Adam's antics. Crazy-Prepared: He has a habit of not throwing things away if they could be useful; as a result, a great deal of useful stuff can be found in the vast and iconic "wall of boxes" at M5 (over 700 at last count). He also has this approach to life experiences; "better save up those skills; you never know when they'll come in handy"—as they often do on this show.

characters.name: Grant Imahara
characters.description: Grant Imahara provided the robotics expertise on the Build Team, building off of his past experience onBattleBots, theStar Warsprequels, and many more besides (unlike Adam and Tory, he also worked onRevenge of the Sith). He was the most outwardly nerdy of the team (most oftenStar Trek, but really anything was fair game), and was brought on to fill the void after Scottie Chapman departed the show. He was also the only member of the Build Team to have been given on-boarding as a show host, likely in reaction to Scottie, Kari and Tory getting none because they started out expecting to only be a fabrication group. Grant died on July 13, 2020, of an intracranial aneursym. The Ace: Is generally considered the best at electronics and robotics among all the Mythbusters. Even Jaime and Adam would come to him for advice on a particular build. The All-Solving Hammer: Sometimes, his "build a robot" suggestion is just silly (or at least played for laughs). Asian and Nerdy: He plays this up for his own (and the audience's) amusement. The guy builds robots onanyexcuse and used pi as his "prisoner number" in the jailhouse rope episode. As if that wasn't bad enough, when they were testing a lie detector machine, it was revealed he had thought about building a female robot. In another episode, he was reduced to a stammering idiot when he met a high-tech bomb disposal robot. This vibe is intensified when you realize his college degree was in electrical engineering.

characters.name: Tory Belleci
characters.description: One of the fabricators for the M7 team, Tory was the more manic and energetic member of the team, and is often the one having the most outright fun on the job. In the earlier seasons he, along with Adam, was defined by being the one who constantly got himself hurt on-camera, to the point that the narrator would note that it's been a while since he did so (usually right before he'd do it again). This toned down in the later series as he learned from these experiences and the team gained a stronger safety regimen. Tory is also the only religious member of the team, being Christian, and had worked with Adam in Industrial Light and Magic similar to Grant. Tory is the second host to return to a later iteration of Mythbusters, being one of the hosts forMotor Mythbusters. Ascended Extra: Originated as a random crew member who got the slightest bit of camera time during the Archimedes Death Ray build (offscreen he had been close friends with Adam for years). He would go on to be paired with Kari as part of the build team, and then graduate to full Mythbuster. Be Careful What You Wish For: Tory will oftenjump at the chanceto do something crazy and possibly dangerous (skydiving, climbing into a rodeo ring with abull), only to quickly go intoThis Is Gonna Suckmode when faced with actuallydoingit.Tory:Skydiving!![later]Tory:Do we *really* need to test this myth? The Big Guy: Of the Build Team members (and possibly the entireMythBusterscrew), because he does the most stunts. When testing among the Mythbusters to get average levels of human strength for a given task, Tory almost always sets the high end.

characters.name: Kari Byron
characters.description: The third member of the Build Team, Kari actually started out in M5 for something completely different: aCADdesigner for Jamie's toy prototyping work, starting the same day Mythbusters started filming. The show caught her attention and she drove herself out to the Mohave desert for the original JATO Car myth, and was later infamously used as the scan model of a woman's butt for another of the pilot myths. This got enough positive reactions for her to be bumped up into the Build Team. Kari is often the one grossed out by what the team have to make and do, but is also the closest thing the show had to a gun nut. Ascended Extra: Her first appearance was being used as a butt mold subject (off-screen, she then used her CAD skills to turn it into the more portly posterior that was actually used for testing). Break the Cutie: She's every bit as badass as her male co-stars, but she was not able to handle being subjected to Chinese water torture. After enduring it for nearly an hour, shebroke down into tearsand had to stop.Smell of Fear also brought her close to the breaking point by being encased in a box with scorpions. It got to the point where her distress caused her foot to shake involuntarilywhile it had a scorpion on it. Smell of Fear also brought her close to the breaking point by being encased in a box with scorpions. It got to the point where her distress caused her foot to shake involuntarilywhile it had a scorpion on it. Distracted by the Sexy: When she wore a silicone-stuffed bra to test whether or not women with larger breasts received more tips, even Grant and Tory admitted they had a hard time keeping their eyes above her neckline.

characters.name: Jessi Combs
characters.description: Jessi Combs was brought on to the Build Team for the 2009-2010 season to fill in for Kari while she was in maternity leave, and stood out for her love of automobiles and welding. Action Girl:Pulled herself up from hanging off of a helicopter's skid and into its cockpit on the first try, and with far greater ease than either Tory or Grant (it didn't hurt that she weighed less than them). Pulled herself up from hanging off of a helicopter's skid and into its cockpit on the first try, and with far greater ease than either Tory or Grant (it didn't hurt that she weighed less than them). Guest-Star Party Member: She was a substitute Mythbuster during Kari's maternity leave. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: Officially Jessi was there as a temporary fill-in for Kari while she was on maternity leave. But she was also a thin blonde with some tattoos and was proficient in welding and vehicle mechanics, which would remind fans of Scottie. She even copied Scottie's shipping-container-into-ramp trick that she did for the 2nd attempt at the JATO Rocket Car myth.

characters.name: Scottie Chapman
characters.description: Scottie was initially hired as a metalwork fabricator who, alongside Christine Chamberlain, helped with making the builds in M5 for Adam and Jamie's myths, as a separate team from Kari and Tory who operated in their own workshop. Her role very quickly expanded to being one of the co-hosts alongside Kari and Tory as all three of them tackled their own myths, making her the original "fifth Mythbuster". Most notably, Scottie was the one who made the original metal "Mythbusters" sign and the Busted, Plausible and Confirmed signs, which continued to be used all the way to the end of Adam and Jamie's run of the show. She also fabricated the metal frames for all of the blast shields that similarly saw continuous use until the end of the original run. The Bus Came Back: Made a guest reappearance in the final setup of the first JATO Rocket revisit. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Mid episode during the rolling stones myth, Scottie vanished without note or explanation, and Grant was slotted into her place. Early-Bird Cameo: Inverted. She appeared a few episodes after her departure, because the rolling stones myth was recorded over several months, and thus was still rolling when she left.

characters.name: Christine Chamberlain
characters.description: Ascended Extra: Ish. She got a little more focus in the second half of her tenure. Butt-Monkey: In three episodes in a row:She got a scolding from Jamie for nearly wrecking his lathe.Had her face burned by a shattering jawbreaker (which also burned Adam's arm)Sliced open her hand with a box cutter. She got a scolding from Jamie for nearly wrecking his lathe. Had her face burned by a shattering jawbreaker (which also burned Adam's arm) Sliced open her hand with a box cutter.

characters.name: Jess Nelson
characters.description: Ascended Extra: She originated from a viewer challenge for Archimedes Death Ray, and impressed the team enough to get a few additional appearances as a mythtern. Deadpan Snarker: Demonstrated this on the few occasions when she spoke on camera, particularly when mocking that Adam put himself through in the course of testing myths. One-Steve Limit: Downplayed, as her time on the show didn't overlap with Jessi's, but fans usually refer to her by her shortened name in order to quickly distinguish the two. The Quiet One: Compared to Christine, her predecessor as Mythtern, she mostly stayed in the background and helped out with the technical aspects of the experiments. What Happened to the Mouse?: When her term as a Mythern ended, she opted to move onto other projects, and the position was never officially filled again during the show's run.

characters.name: Buster
characters.description: The team's crash test dummy, naturally used in place of real humans for all sorts of experiments no real person could or should attempt. Originally your standard crash test dummy, the Mythbusters upgraded him in Season 2 to be more easily repairable, and gave him durability of that more like a human adult male. These upgrades include a cast aluminum skeleton, poplar wood bones, and flesh and muscles of “Dragon Skin” silicone rubber. Over time however even these upgrades would fail all to frequently, resulting in "Buster" becoming the name of any dummy used for a given experiment for cost's sake. Despite this, special attention is called when the original model does make an appearance. Ace Custom: Depending on the needs of the myth the team has modified Buster to provide more relative data on the level of impact and damage from an incident. Joints were replaced so they could position him in different contraptions. Strips of birch were included as an analog to human bones to see if it will break from impact. Plus the items they swapped out were designed to be more easily replaceable with local hardware compared to the proprietary stuff from the original. Ascended to a Higher Plane of Existence: Invoked andPlayed for Laughsin the finale, in what is about the closest to the trope a real-life person could conceivably come. Buster is dressed up as a superhero, positioned on a rocket sled aimed at a concrete wall, and launched at it at 800 mph. The sheer power of the impact, estimated to be over five million joules of energy,disintegratesBuster in an instant — there's a few flashes of light as he hits the wall and then he just ceases to be, the only thing left behind being a few shreds of his costume and some tiny pieces of debris. The narrator milks the "mysterious" vanishing for all it's worth, and Adam cracks that Buster went to Mythbuster Valhalla, and he could not conceive a more worthy reward for his years of service. Butt-Monkey: Buster's job is to suffer in the nam

characters.name: Robert Lee
characters.description: Admit it. You read the opening quote to this page in his voice. Yet another iconic part of the show, Robert is the only cast member to be present across every season and iteration of Mythbusters.noteThat being the original run,The Searchand Season 11,Mythbusters Junior,Motor MythbustersandThere's Your Problem! Added Alliterative Appeal: AtDrinking Gameproportions. One of the most common is "a classic car / cinematic / cartoon conundrum". Deadpan Snarker: On occasion. Usually with sarcasm in response to the gang’s antics. Interactive Narrator: Usually with Adam, who is most likely to refer directly to him, especially later in the series run. In Fireball Stun Gun, Adam directly refers to Robert by name as a cue to tell the winning shirt material "what they've won" like a game show co-host. Rhymes on a Dime: Though not as often as thepunsor thealliteration, this occasionally finds its way into his narration as well.

characters.name: Heather Joseph-Witham
characters.description: A folklorist from the three pilots and Season 1, Heather was called upon to explain the myth itself (often intercutting with Adam and Jamie's explanation over a blueprint), as well as its cultural impact and why the myth has perpetuated. Despite appearing in the M5 shop in Season 1, she's never seen directly interacting with any other cast member. Early-Installment Weirdness: Her presence explaining the myths and their social significance lends the show to feel much different from later episodes. Later episodes would have this exposition provided either in the Blueprint Room segments or in the animatics, and the "experts" would be visited and interviewed directly by one of the team instead. Ms. Exposition: Her role as Folklorist. Heather is the person who will explain the myth being tested, including (if necessary) the social significance, or why it became so prominent. Put on a Bus: As the show's focus shifted to the mechanics of testing the myths rather than the social significance, her presence was eventually phased out by the end of 2005.

characters.name: Frank Doyle
characters.description: A retired FBI agent who assists the team with some of their more explosive myths. He was the first true explosives expert on the show, and is how the Mythbusters were able to get their hands on someseriouslydangerous stuff. Bash Brothers: When paired with J.D. Nelson. Deadpan Snarker: He isverydeadpan.Tory:Frank, why are you standing so far away?Frank:Because I want to continue to live. Establishing Character Moment: The point when he's truly cemented astheexplosives expert for the show comes in, ironically enough, his first appearance in the "Cement Mix-Up" myth. With the team having had trouble with various pyrotechnicians hired to cause explosions in prior episodes (and even earlier in the testing of the same myth), Frank immediately establishes his expertise when Adam and Jamie ask him where the best place to observe the explosion would be; he swiftly replies that with an explosion of that size, youdon'tobserve it unless you're willing to take the risk of being killed by flying debris, even the tiniest piece of which can be as devastating as a bullet. Retired Badass: He's a former FBI special agent.

characters.name: Sgt. J.D. Nelson
characters.description: A member of the Alameda County Sheriff's Department bomb squad, J.D. usually appears whenever the team is blowing something up at the department's bomb squad training range. His debut is in fact thanks to Frank Doyle, who reached out to him for a myth that required his facility, and the rest is history. J.D. is also the only on-camera member of the original series to appear inMythbusters Junior(aside from Adam, of course), courtesy of them needing to use the bomb range yet again. The Alcoholic: While he doesn't have a drinking problem, he's always saddest when theMythBustersdestroy or discard alcohol.J.D.:On the record, I'm just gonna say I'm totally against this burning of wine and champagne. Bash Brothers: When paired with Frank Doyle. Deadpan Snarker: Compared with Frank, not quite as deadpan, but a bit snarkier.J.D.:[after Jamie insists on making aCartoon Bombout of the C4 for their experiment]I have no doubt in my mindyou're gonna get that Road Runnerthis time. Stuff Blowing Up: His job is to make this occur without crossing into "PeopleBlowing Up". When not working with theMythBusters, his job is the disposal of bombs, old military ordinance, and suspicious packages.
